rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, August 06, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION's Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement & Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation. Judge Miller is recused. Per Order of the Presiding Judge, this matter will be heard by Judge Kramer in Dept. 303. rulings.Case Title: EDWARD F. WOCH VS. ECOLOGY AND ENVIRONMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Pltf Edward Woch And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 6, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT ECOLOGY AND ENVIRONMENT, INC., RON KARPOWICZ, DAVE PLUMPTON Notice Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Edward Woch And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT, ECOLOGY AND ENVIRONMENT, INC'S ("ENE") MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION IN DEFENDANT COUNSEL'S OAKLAND OFFICE ON OR BEFORE SEPTEMBER 5TH IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FILED HIS COMPLAINT IN SAN FRANCISCO, CA. AND RESIDED IN CALIFORNIA AT THE TIMES THAT DEFENDANT INITIALLY SOUGHT TO TAKE HIS DEPOSITION. THERE WAS NO LEGAL JUSTIFICATION FOR HIS REFUSAL TO APPEAR FOR HIS PREVIOUSLY NOTICED DEPOSITION BASED ON HIS ASSERTION THAT ENE WAS REQUIRED TO COMPLY WITH HIS DISCOVERY REQUESTS BEFORE HE COULD BE COMPELLED TO APPEAR FOR HIS DEPOSITION. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000. WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF, PAYMENT OF WHICH SHALL BE MADE TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDERS SUBMITTED WITH ITS MOTIONS EXCEPT AS MODIFIED AS TO THE AMOUNT OF SANCTIONS. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN LIGGETT VS. SAUSE BROS. INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Disqualify Deft'S Expert
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 6, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF JOHN LIGGETT Motion To Disqualify Defendant's Expert. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIF JOHN LIGGETT'S MOTION TO DISQUALIFY DEFENDANT, SAUSE BROS, INC.'S EXPERT WITNESS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT IT WOULD BE FUNDEMENTALLY UNFAIR TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF, BY REASON OF HIS COUNSEL'S FAILURE TO CORRECTLY IDENTIFY THE DEFENDANT IN THIS MATTER, TO DISQUALIFY DEFENDANT'S EXPERT WITNESS WHO IT HAD RETAINED ALMOST TWO YEARS PRIOR TO THE CONTACT BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. THE EXTENT AND QUALITY OF THE CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ASSSERTS WAS SHARED WITH THE DOCTOR DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE MATERIAL ENOUGH TO JUSTIFY PRECLUDING DEFENDANT FROM USING DR. HAYES AS AN EXPERT WITNESS. IT APPEARS TO BE UNCONTRADICTED THAT PLAINTIFF.S COUNSEL WAS ADVISED PRIOR TO HIS CONTACT WITH DR. HAYES OFFICE THAT DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL HAD USED DR. HAYES IN PRIOR MATTERS. THIS SHOULD HAVE CAUSED HIM TO BE PARTICULARLY CAREFUL IN ASCERTAINING THAT DR. HAYES HAD NO CONFLICT. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DOREEN SINGH VS. EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Special Interrogatories (Set One) Nos 1 And 2 And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 6, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF DOREEN SINGH Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories (Set One) Nos. 1 And 2 And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF DOREEN SINGH'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 & 2 IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: 1. DEFENDANT, WITHIN TEN (10) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER WILL PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS 1 & 2 LIMITED TO COVERED EMPLOYEES EMPLOYED IN DEFENDANT'S OFFICE AT 555 MONTGOMERY ST., SAN FRANCISCO, CA. 2. THE COURT WILL ASSESS NO MONETARY SANCTIONS DUE TO PLAINTIFF'S MINIMAL EFFORTS TO MEET AND CONFER. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TO APPROVE AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT THE ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AT THE HEARING ON AUGUST 6, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
